revision.text:
[[de:Cheret-waret]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W12-D21:X1 D56-D21:X1 N14-HASH |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Cheret-waret
''ẖr.t wˁrt''
''The One Below the Thigh (of Sah)'' }} '''Cheret-waret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qid=Q2211961|1=Sah (constellation)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qid=Q142353|1=Star clock}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|qid=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qid=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}} associated with this decan is the outer {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} of Orion’s Belt, {{ill|Alnitak|en|Alnitak|qid=Q13076|lt=Alnitak|1=Alnitak}}, a {{ill|Blue giant|en|Blue giant|qid=Q5855|lt=Blue giant|1=Blue giant}} in the constellation {{ill|Orion (constellation)|en|Orion (constellation)|qid=Q8860|lt=Orion (constellation)|1=Orion (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qid=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Cheret-waret represented the 33rd decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qid=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret III|de|26. Peret III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 94. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Cheret-waret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:チェレット・ワレット]] [[de:Cheret-waret]] {{wikidata link|Q1070217}}
